info.title: Luma API
info.description:
API for interacting with Luma's event platform.

## Rate Limits

The Luma API uses a per-minute rate limit shared across `GET` and `POST` requests:

- **Calendar API keys** and OAuth tokens: **200 requests/minute** per calendar.
- **Organization API keys**: **500 requests/minute** per organization.

When you exceed the limit the API returns `429 Too Many Requests`. Back off before retrying.

parameters.name: event_id

schema.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

parameters.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

get.description: Return admin information about an event that you have manage access for.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the event location. Null for online events or when the address can't be geocoded.

location_type.description: `offline` for in-person events. Online events have the meeting platform (`zoom`, `meet`, etc.), or `unknown` if we don't recognize the meeting link. `missing` if the event has no location set.

location_visibility.description: Whether the event's address is shown to everyone or only to approved guests. This API always returns the full address.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

waitlist_status.description: `enabled` if the event has a waitlist. New registrations join the waitlist once the event reaches capacity.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

region.description: State or province, e.g. 'New York' or 'California'.

country.description: Country name, e.g. 'United States'. Null for cities in disputed territories where Google omits the country.

timezone.description: IANA timezone of the city, e.g. 'America/New_York'.

location.description: City the calendar is based in (e.g. for city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the calendar's primary location (e.g. city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

get.description:
Every event and API key on Luma is managed by a [Luma Calendar](https://help.luma.com/p/luma-calendar-overview). You can list all of the events managed by your Calendar by using this API route.

Note that this API route will not list events that are listed on the Calendar but not managed by the Calendar.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the event location. Null for online events or when the address can't be geocoded.

location_type.description: `offline` for in-person events. Online events have the meeting platform (`zoom`, `meet`, etc.), or `unknown` if we don't recognize the meeting link. `missing` if the event has no location set.

location_visibility.description: Whether the event's address is shown to everyone or only to approved guests. This API always returns the full address.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

waitlist_status.description: `enabled` if the event has a waitlist. New registrations join the waitlist once the event reaches capacity.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the event location. Null for online events or when the address can't be geocoded.

get.description:
Get detailed information for an event guest by looking them up by their ID. This supports looking them up by different parameters.

This response includes `event_ticket_orders`, which includes `coupon_info` when a coupon was applied.

invited_at.description: When the guest was invited to the event. Null if they registered on their own.

joined_at.description: When the guest first clicked the virtual join link (luma.com/join/...). Only set for online events. Null if the guest has not joined.

registered_at.description: When the guest registered for the event.

utm_source.description: The UTM source parameter captured when the guest registered (from the `?utm_source=` query parameter).

coupon_info.description: The coupon that was applied to this ticket order, if any.

event_ticket_orders.description: Detailed ticket orders for this guest. Use this to inspect applied coupon_info per order.

get.description:
Get list of guests who have registered or been invited to an event.

This list response includes guest summaries and `event_tickets`, but not order-level details like `event_ticket_orders`. Use `/v1/events/guests/get` when you need detailed ticket order data for a specific guest.

invited_at.description: When the guest was invited to the event. Null if they registered on their own.

joined_at.description: When the guest first clicked the virtual join link (luma.com/join/...). Only set for online events. Null if the guest has not joined.

registered_at.description: When the guest registered for the event.

utm_source.description: The UTM source parameter captured when the guest registered (from the `?utm_source=` query parameter).

get.description: List all events across all calendars belonging to the organization. Events are deduplicated if they appear on multiple calendars within the organization.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the event location. Null for online events or when the address can't be geocoded.

location_type.description: `offline` for in-person events. Online events have the meeting platform (`zoom`, `meet`, etc.), or `unknown` if we don't recognize the meeting link. `missing` if the event has no location set.

location_visibility.description: Whether the event's address is shown to everyone or only to approved guests. This API always returns the full address.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

waitlist_status.description: `enabled` if the event has a waitlist. New registrations join the waitlist once the event reaches capacity.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

zoom_meeting_url.description: Use `meeting_url` instead. Not all meetings use Zoom.

region.description: State or province, e.g. 'New York' or 'California'.

country.description: Country name, e.g. 'United States'. Null for cities in disputed territories where Google omits the country.

timezone.description: IANA timezone of the city, e.g. 'America/New_York'.

location.description: City the calendar is based in (e.g. for city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the calendar's primary location (e.g. city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

status.description: Status of the event on this calendar.

managing_calendars.description: Calendars with manage access to this event, with the event's status on each. Calendars that only list the event without manage access are not included.

cover_url.description: Specify an image that has been uploaded to the Luma CDN. You can upload an image after getting an upload URL from the Luma API.

description_md.description: Luma stores rich text in a special format called Spark. We don't expose an API for that format, but we can convert Markdown to Spark for you. Some Spark features, like Luma event embeds, are not supported by Markdown. Images are supported using standard markdown syntax (![alt](url)) — image URLs must be hosted on the Luma CDN (images.lumacdn.com). Upload images via the /v1/images/create-upload-url endpoint first.

max_capacity.description:
Once an event hits the max capacity, the event will be marked as sold out and registration will automatically be closed.

If `can_register_for_multiple_tickets` is `true`, each ticket will count towards the max capacity.

name_requirement.description: Luma collects the name of every guest as they register. Choose if you'd like to split this into two fields to encourage guests to enter their first and last name.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

reminders_disabled.description: By default, Luma will send reminders before the event. Turn off if you'd like more control over event communications.

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests. If not specified on create, inherits from calendar defaults.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

show_guest_list.description: When the guest list is shown, approved guests will be able to see who else is going to the event.

slug.description:
The URL slug for the event. If the slug is `aloha`, the URL will be `https://luma.com/aloha`. If the slug is not available, the event creation will fail.

You can use `/public/v1/entity/lookup` to check if a slug is available.

tint_color.description: A hex color like '#bb2dc7'. Alpha channels (#rgba, #rrggbbaa) are automatically stripped.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

suppress_notifications.description: If true, Luma will not notify guests (via email or push notification) when updating the event name, time, or location.

cover_url.description: Specify an image that has been uploaded to the Luma CDN. You can upload an image after getting an upload URL from the Luma API.

description_md.description: Luma stores rich text in a special format called Spark. We don't expose an API for that format, but we can convert Markdown to Spark for you. Some Spark features, like Luma event embeds, are not supported by Markdown. Images are supported using standard markdown syntax (![alt](url)) — image URLs must be hosted on the Luma CDN (images.lumacdn.com). Upload images via the /v1/images/create-upload-url endpoint first.

max_capacity.description:
Once an event hits the max capacity, the event will be marked as sold out and registration will automatically be closed.

If `can_register_for_multiple_tickets` is `true`, each ticket will count towards the max capacity.

name_requirement.description: Luma collects the name of every guest as they register. Choose if you'd like to split this into two fields to encourage guests to enter their first and last name.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

reminders_disabled.description: By default, Luma will send reminders before the event. Turn off if you'd like more control over event communications.

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests. If not specified on create, inherits from calendar defaults.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

show_guest_list.description: When the guest list is shown, approved guests will be able to see who else is going to the event.

slug.description:
The URL slug for the event. If the slug is `aloha`, the URL will be `https://luma.com/aloha`. If the slug is not available, the event creation will fail.

You can use `/public/v1/entity/lookup` to check if a slug is available.

tint_color.description: A hex color like '#bb2dc7'. Alpha channels (#rgba, #rrggbbaa) are automatically stripped.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Update a guest’s status. Set `status` to `approved` ("Going"), `declined`, `pending_approval`, or `waitlist`. Use `should_refund` to refund a paid guest when moving them out of an approved state. Use `message` to include a personal note in the email we send the guest. To send a soft invite that guests can accept, use `/v1/events/guests/send-invites` instead.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

guest_id.description: Guest identifier — the guest ID (gst-), a ticket key, a guest key (g-), or the user's email.

should_refund.description: If you are moving a guest who has paid to `declined`, `waitlist`, or `pending_approval`, should we refund their payment? Defaults to false.

send_email.description: Optional. Whether Luma should email the guest about the status change. Defaults to true. Set to false if your integration sends its own notifications.

message.description: Optional. Personal message included in the email we send the guest about the status change. Max of 200 characters. Can't be combined with `send_email: false`.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Send guests an invite to an event. We'll send an email, and if their phone number is linked to their Luma account, they'll also receive an SMS.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

guests.description: Information about the guests you want to invite to the event. If a user already has a name, this will not overwrite their name.

message.description: Personalize the message that will be included in the invite. Max of 200 characters.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Add guests to the event. By default, guests are added with the status "Going" (approved) and receive one ticket of the default ticket type. Use the `ticket` or `tickets` parameter to specify custom ticket assignments. Get available ticket types using `/v1/events/ticket-types/list`. Use `approval_status` to add guests as pending approval or on the waitlist instead of immediately approved.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

items.description: Information about the guests you want to add to the event. If a user already has a name, this will not overwrite their name.

ticket.description: Optional. Assign a single ticket of the specified type to each guest. Cannot be used with `tickets` parameter.

tickets.description: Optional. Assign multiple tickets to each guest. Each guest receives all tickets in the array. Use this to add multiple tickets of the same or different types. Cannot be used with `ticket` parameter.

approval_status.description: Optional. Status to assign to each added guest. Defaults to `approved` ("Going"). Use `pending_approval` to add guests for host review, or `waitlist` to add to the event waitlist. To send a soft invite that guests can accept, use `/v1/events/guests/send-invites` instead.

send_email.description: Optional. Whether Luma should email each added guest. Defaults to true. Set to false if your integration sends its own notifications.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Add a host to an event. This works for both managers and check-in staff — use the access_level parameter to control permissions.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

name.description: Name of the host you are adding. If they already have a Luma profile, this will be ignored.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Update a host's permissions or visibility on an event. Use this to change a host between check-in and manager access levels. The event creator's access level cannot be changed.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

email.description: Email of the host to update.

access_level.description: The new access level for the host. One of "none", "check-in", or "manager". The event creator’s access level cannot be changed.

is_visible.description: Whether the host is visible on the event page.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Remove a host from an event. The event creator cannot be removed.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

email.description: Email of the host to remove.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Create a coupon that can be applied when a guest is registering for an event. Restrict it to a ticket type with `event_ticket_type_id` — when that ticket type is hidden, this creates an unlock (access) code. You are not able to edit the coupon terms after it has been created.

code.description: This is the code that the user will enter on the event page. It is not case sensitive. Maximum 20 characters.

remaining_count.description: Number of times the coupon can be used. Set to 1000000 for unlimited uses.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

event_ticket_type_id.description: Optional. Restrict the coupon to a single ticket type. If that ticket type is hidden, the coupon acts as an unlock (access) code that reveals the ticket during registration. Pair a hidden ticket type that has Require Approval turned off with an unlock code to let invited guests register and pay directly, skipping the Apply to Attend step. For a code that unlocks the ticket without changing the price, set the discount to 0% (`percent_off: 0`).
The ticket type this coupon is restricted to, if any. Set for unlock (access) codes that reveal a hidden ticket type.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Create a coupon that can be applied to any event that is managed by the calendar. Be careful not to have the same code on an event and on the calendar.

code.description: This is the code that the user will enter on the event page. It is not case sensitive. Maximum 20 characters.

remaining_count.description: Number of times the coupon can be used. Set to 1000000 for unlimited uses.

event_ticket_type_id.description: The ticket type this coupon is restricted to, if any. Set for unlock (access) codes that reveal a hidden ticket type.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Import contacts to your calendar to easily invite them to events and send them newsletters.

contacts.description: Information about the contacts you want to import. You can include a name and email. Note that we will not set the name of a user once it has already been set.

tags.description: Tag IDs or tag names to apply to the contacts.

schema.description: Empty response

id.description: Tag ID, this usually starts with tag-

tag_id.description: Tag ID, this usually starts with tag-

schema.description: Empty response

tag_id.description: Tag ID, this usually starts with tag-

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Apply a tag to existing calendar contacts. This will not create new contacts.

user_ids.description: Array of user IDs to apply the tag to

emails.description: Array of email addresses to apply the tag to

applied_count.description: Number of contacts the tag was successfully applied to

skipped_count.description: Number of users skipped because they are not calendar contacts

post.description: Unapply a tag from calendar contacts. Only affects existing contacts.

user_ids.description: Array of user IDs to remove the tag from

emails.description: Array of email addresses to remove the tag from

removed_count.description: Number of contacts the tag was successfully removed from

skipped_count.description: Number of users skipped because they are not calendar contacts

id.description: Tag ID, this usually starts with tag-

tag_id.description: Tag ID, this usually starts with tag-

schema.description: Empty response

tag_id.description: Tag ID, this usually starts with tag-

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Apply a tag to events on the calendar.

event_ids.description: Array of event IDs to apply the tag to

applied_count.description: Number of events the tag was successfully applied to

skipped_count.description: Number of events skipped because they are not on this calendar

post.description: Unapply a tag from events on the calendar.

event_ids.description: Array of event IDs to remove the tag from

removed_count.description: Number of events the tag was successfully removed from

skipped_count.description: Number of events skipped because they are not on this calendar

post.description:
Add or submit an existing event (on Luma or on an external platform) to the Luma calendar. By default, events added by a calendar manager are approved immediately. Use `submission_mode: "pending"` to keep the submission pending.

On calendars with `require-manage-access`, submitting a Luma event you manage can also grant the target calendar manage access once the submission is approved.

submission_mode.description: `auto` approves the event immediately. `pending` keeps the event pending for calendar admin approval.
`auto` approves the event immediately. `pending` keeps the event pending for calendar admin approval.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

post.description: Approve a pending event submission on your calendar. The event becomes visible on the calendar and the submitter is notified.

calendar_event_id.description: Calendar event ID (starts with `calev-`). You can also pass the Luma event ID (starts with `evt-`) — we'll resolve it to the active submission on your calendar.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Reject a pending event submission on your calendar. If the event is still pending, the submitter is notified.

calendar_event_id.description: Calendar event ID (starts with `calev-`). You can also pass the Luma event ID (starts with `evt-`) — we'll resolve it to the active submission on your calendar.

message.description: Optional message to send to the submitter explaining the rejection.

schema.description: Empty response

upload_url.description: You'll upload your image here using a PUT request.

post.description: Create a new ticket type for an event

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

post.description: Update an existing ticket type configuration

event_ticket_type_id.description: Ticket type ID, this usually starts with ttype-

post.description: Soft delete a ticket type. Cannot delete if tickets have been sold or if it's the last visible ticket type.

event_ticket_type_id.description: Ticket type ID, this usually starts with ttype-

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Add a user to a membership tier. For paid tiers, set skip_payment to true if you are handling payment externally.

skip_payment.description: Set to true for paid tiers when handling payment externally. Required for paid tiers.

post.description: Update a member's membership status. Approving a paid tier member captures their payment. Declining cancels any active subscription.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Create a new webhook endpoint to receive event notifications.
Update a webhook endpoint's event types or status (active/paused).
Delete a webhook endpoint.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Request a cancellation token for an event. Canceling an event is irreversible — it notifies all guests, processes refunds, and permanently deletes the event. Because of this, cancellation uses a two-step flow: first request a token here, then pass it to /events/cancel to execute. The token expires after 15 minutes.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

cancellation_token.description: A short-lived token to confirm the cancellation. Expires after 15 minutes.

is_paid.description: Whether the event has paid guests. If true, you must pass should_refund in the confirm step.

guest_count.description: The number of approved guests for this event.

post.description: Cancel an event. You must first call /events/cancel/request to get a cancellation_token, then pass it here. This is irreversible — the event will be deleted, all guests will be notified, and refunds will be processed if should_refund is true. For paid events, you must specify should_refund.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

cancellation_token.description: The cancellation token returned from the request endpoint.

should_refund.description: Whether to refund paid guests. Required if the event has paid guests.

schema.description: Empty response

post.description: Update a calendar's settings. Only the provided fields are changed.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID, this usually starts with cal-.

slug.description: URL slug for the calendar page (e.g. 'my-community' → lu.ma/my-community).

avatar_url.description: Specify an image that has been uploaded to the Luma CDN.

tint_color.description: Tint color for the calendar in hex format (e.g. '#E3CBEF').

region.description: State or province, e.g. 'New York' or 'California'.

country.description: Country name, e.g. 'United States'. Null for cities in disputed territories where Google omits the country.

timezone.description: IANA timezone of the city, e.g. 'America/New_York'.

location.description: City the calendar is based in (e.g. for city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the calendar's primary location (e.g. city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

post.description: Create a new calendar within the organization. The calendar will be managed by the API key owner.

slug.description: URL slug for the calendar page (e.g. 'my-community' → lu.ma/my-community).

avatar_url.description: Specify an image that has been uploaded to the Luma CDN. If not provided, a random default avatar will be used.

tint_color.description: Tint color for the calendar in hex format (e.g. '#E3CBEF'). If not provided, a random color matching the default avatar will be used.

region.description: State or province, e.g. 'New York' or 'California'.

country.description: Country name, e.g. 'United States'. Null for cities in disputed territories where Google omits the country.

timezone.description: IANA timezone of the city, e.g. 'America/New_York'.

location.description: City the calendar is based in (e.g. for city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the calendar's primary location (e.g. city calendars). Null when the calendar isn't tied to a place.

post.description: Transfer an event from its current managing calendar to a different calendar within the organization. The event's managing calendar changes to the destination calendar. Both the source and destination calendars must belong to the organization.

event_id.description: Event ID, this usually starts with evt-

calendar_id.description: Destination calendar ID, this usually starts with cal-. Must belong to the organization.

schema.description: Empty response

apiKeyAuth.name: x-luma-api-key
apiKeyAuth.description: API key to authenticate requests to the Luma API. Generate an API key on your Luma dashboard.

post.description: Triggered when a new event is created on your calendar.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the event location. Null for online events or when the address can't be geocoded.

location_type.description: `offline` for in-person events. Online events have the meeting platform (`zoom`, `meet`, etc.), or `unknown` if we don't recognize the meeting link. `missing` if the event has no location set.

location_visibility.description: Whether the event's address is shown to everyone or only to approved guests. This API always returns the full address.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

waitlist_status.description: `enabled` if the event has a waitlist. New registrations join the waitlist once the event reaches capacity.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

200.description: Return a 2xx status to acknowledge receipt of the webhook.

post.description: Triggered when a guest registers for an event, including re-registrations after cancellation.

invited_at.description: When the guest was invited to the event. Null if they registered on their own.

joined_at.description: When the guest first clicked the virtual join link (luma.com/join/...). Only set for online events. Null if the guest has not joined.

registered_at.description: When the guest registered for the event.

utm_source.description: The UTM source parameter captured when the guest registered (from the `?utm_source=` query parameter).

coupon_info.description: The coupon that was applied to this ticket order, if any.

event_ticket_orders.description: Detailed ticket orders for this guest. Use this to inspect applied coupon_info per order.

coordinate.description: Latitude and longitude of the event location. Null for online events or when the address can't be geocoded.

location_type.description: `offline` for in-person events. Online events have the meeting platform (`zoom`, `meet`, etc.), or `unknown` if we don't recognize the meeting link. `missing` if the event has no location set.

location_visibility.description: Whether the event's address is shown to everyone or only to approved guests. This API always returns the full address.

content_md.description: Markdown content for the terms. Luma converts this to rich text internally.

require_review.description: When true, guests must open the hosted terms before accepting.

waitlist_status.description: `enabled` if the event has a waitlist. New registrations join the waitlist once the event reaches capacity.

enabled.description: Whether to send the feedback email.

delay.description: How long after the event ends to send the feedback email. Defaults to PT0M (immediately). Maximum is P7D (7 days).

feedback_email.description: Settings for the post-event feedback email sent to guests.

200.description: Return a 2xx status to acknowledge receipt of the webhook.
